counters._correction_note: First version of this file divided counter totals by N=200 instead of N×RUNS=1000, inflating per-button counters 5×. Wall-clock + mountChild numbers were always correct.

decision_notes: Wall-clock collapsed = 2.3% of baseline (target: ≤30%, exceeds by 13×). mountChild collapsed = 1.0 per visible (target: ≤1.5, exact). Hypothesis dramatically validated. Next step is NOT another experiment — it's a compiler-pass RFC for `@pyreon/compiler` that detects literal-prop rocketstyle call sites and emits `_tpl()` cloneNode with pre-resolved class strings. Honest caveat: 44× is the upper bound; a real compiler must handle reactive props, event delegation, and dynamic dimensions, falling through to the regular path for non-resolvable sites. Even at 30%-resolvable + 44× win + identity-on-rest, weighted improvement is ~6× — still enormous. Secondary finding (with the corrected per-button math): each Button mount fires 22 styler.resolve + 21 unistyle.descriptor calls — ~40µs aggregate per mount, half the wall-clock cost of the rocketstyle path. Worth investigating regardless of the compiler-collapse RFC.
